timeline.label: Asbury Park seller-financed venture deconsolidated after $10.6M mezzanine repayment; simplified balance sheet and released guaranty.
Q1 2026: SAFE stake marked $183.0M (~13.5M shares); total debt $207.0M; cash and restricted cash $62.1M.
Management fee stepped down to $10.0M annual term; current term $7.5M through March 2027.
FY2025 share repurchases ~$8M; active buyback program continues into 2026.
First full year post-spin management fee $25.0M; wind-down fee schedule begins stepping down.
Star Holdings spun from iStar/Safehold merger; legacy non-ground-lease assets separated; ~13.5M SAFE shares retained.
SAFE closed ~$25.80 on merger day; deal-mark anchor for stake cross-check.
Q1 2026 repurchased ~0.2M shares for $2.0M; continued capital return via buybacks not dividends.

industry.structure: STHO is a wind-down holding vehicle, not an operating REIT franchise. Value is driven by asset marks, loan repayments, land sales, and a large Safehold equity stake; externally managed by a Safehold subsidiary.
industry.share_shift: Legacy iStar assets continue monetizing (MD&A schedule down to $71.4M carrying); Magnolia Green lot sales and Asbury simplification reduce complexity; no operating market share dynamics apply.
industry.trend: Rate-sensitive SAFE mark, margin-loan constraints on the stake, and related-party management fees remain the dominant industry-adjacent forces; land/lot sales continue above cost of sales.
